Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Classify a production change as Standard (fully automated, pre-approved), Normal (peer-reviewed PR/MR, delegated approval), Significant (affects High Impact services, formal Change Record), or Emergency (Incident Commander authorized); apply the criteria to determine the correct class

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Complete a change record with all required fields: description, impacted services/CIs, impact, urgency, risk assessment, test results, implementation plan, change reason, communications plan, backout plan, test plan, maintenance window, and evidence

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Explain that Normal changes use peer review + automated tests as primary approval; Significant changes require Change Authority approval and rollback plan; Emergency changes require Incident Commander approval with subsequent review

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Know that each user-facing service has a weekly maintenance window and participates in a monthly company calendar; understand that changes outside windows require justification and approval; notify stakeholders before and after impacting changes

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Verify Normal, Significant, and Emergency changes and update configuration records; conduct a retrospective within five business days when needed; capture lessons learned in the improvement register

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Explain that change freezes may be applied during critical delivery periods or when error budgets are exceeded; only stability-improving fixes are permitted during a freeze
